home.title: Nes HaTamar
home.subtitle: A Journey Through the Paths of Our Sacred Torah to the World of Homeopathy and Medicinal Plants
home.description: Nes HaTamar is a rare fusion of spiritual and material worlds, faith and medicine. It is a soulful journey inviting the reader to know the Healer of all flesh and the life force inherent in humanity.
home.journey: Like a walk through an enchanted garden, the book unveils a rich world of plants, animals, minerals, and metals, revealing the hidden healing powers that operate according to the law of similars: like cures like.
home.connection: This is a pioneering work connecting the Torah of Israel with the world of homeopathy and medicinal plants. The book illuminates how true healing does not merely cover symptoms but touches the root of illness and sweetens its cause.
home.experience: Accompanied by ancient and modern artwork, Nes HaTamar is an experience that engages all senses - internal and external - and opens a new dialogue between the supernatural world of miracles and the natural world itself, between the bitterness of illness and the sweetness of the date palm.

items.name: Hedva Sternberg
items.title: Homeopath, Jerusalem
items.quote: I just received the book. I was so moved by the weight of the package, and when I opened it I was astonished! Everything you told and shared, and everything you showed of the book in the videos, did not reflect the book itself at all. First, its appearance is phenomenally beautiful, with wonderful taste in the combination of colors - the gold on the white fabric with the purple flower. And within the text, the lovely, complementary blend of the heading colors and the fonts. The images are simply a masterpiece, a work of art in both appearance and depth. You can see how the book is written like interwoven vessels of intellect and emotion, the material and the spiritual, in an amazing way. No words! God has touched you. I wish that your wellsprings spread far and wide.

items.name: Miriam Bat-El Amsali
items.title: A'eira Shachar
items.quote: Hello Tamar, the book reached us a few weeks ago. It is amazing and beautifully crafted, and we have already had the chance to immerse ourselves in it several times and learn so much! Thank you very much.

items.name: Keren Frisch
items.title: Zichron Ya'akov
items.quote: Today the book arrived at my home, and it was very moving. At every stage and on every page you can see how much thought, knowledge, investment, and heart you put into it. Homeopathy is a field you have been studying and delving into deeply for many years, and I am certain that with this book you will succeed in making it accessible to the whole world. This is not only a professional and impressive work - it is a work with a mission, the fruit of your labor, the fruit of Nes HaTamar. Well done for the dedication, the courage, and the privilege. Thank you very much.

items.name: Iris Ada
items.title: Ein Hod
items.quote: Dear Tamar, the book Nes HaTamar has reached me, and I want to thank you and express my deep appreciation for the rich content and the meticulous design. A rare book in many respects. Wishing you success and blessing. I will certainly recommend it!

items.name: Hadas Bachar
items.title: Geva Carmel
items.quote: This morning I received your and Nissim's amazing book, Nes HaTamar, and it is simply wonderful and uniquely beautiful. From the very first glance you can feel how much love, thought, and inspiration it holds. I have no doubt I will learn a great deal from it, and I am already looking forward to starting to read and delve into it. Thank you both for this moving creation.

items.name: Dalit Zarankin
items.title: Zichron Ya'akov
items.quote: The book is amazing. On Shabbat I read it for about two and a half hours and dove right in. I don't know why, but I dove into the entire chapter on metals. A true gem to have at home.

items.name: Osnat Barel
items.title: Kfar Saba
items.quote: Dear Tamar, yesterday I received your and Nissim's exquisite, magnificent book, and I cannot remember the last time I opened a book with such excitement and attentiveness, knowing this is a book you leaf through gently when you can devote yourself to it alone, a book to be cherished and guarded, holding for me all the goodness of the earth, the flora and the fauna, and the age-old traditions that connect a person - body, soul, and spirit - to nature. Thank you for the kind blessings.

items.name: Avraham Dahan
items.title: Historian, author, compiler of the Talmud of Plants Encyclopedia and founder of the Talmud of Plants Orchard at Mitzpe HaYamim
items.quote: To the authors of Nes HaTamar, I have received your wonderful book and was deeply impressed by the dedication, knowledge, and love woven into it. It is clearly a work of great value, one that will give readers inspiration, knowledge, and important insights in the fields of health and way of life. My warm congratulations on the book's publication, and great success in spreading its message to the wider public. With warm regards, Avraham Dahan.

items.name: Hana Yoster
items.title: Holon
items.quote: A wondrous, unique book whose content is infinite wisdom. A masterpiece that combines the wisdom of Judaism, the wisdom of nature, and the wisdom of homeopathy - together creating a new, rich, and fascinating cosmos. The book, printed in Italy and a work of art in its own right, incorporates paintings by renowned artists, poetry, sources, and nature photography.

items.name: Edna Zomer
items.title: Hadera
items.quote: Dear Tamar, the wondrous book I have awaited for so long has just arrived. I want to say that I am truly overwhelmed by the magnitude and beauty of the creation, by its appearance and by its profound, all-encompassing content. A book that touches the heart and soul in every sense. I thank you and Nissim with all my heart for this gift for life.

items.name: Nisan Perez
items.title: Historian, independent photography curator, and producer of photography and art books (including for the Israel Museum)
items.quote: Hi Tamar, I received the book with gratitude. You have done monumental work. Congratulations and continued success.

about.title: About the Authors
about.subtitle: Meet the authors of Nes HaTamar

tamar.name: Tamar Eshel
tamar.bio: A lawyer by training, but her heart is devoted to the world of kindness and natural medicine. She studied homeopathy at the school of Yosef Reves, the father of homeopathy in Israel, and for over two decades has been practicing homeopathy and the connection between body, soul, and faith. A graduate of Rabbi Yitzchak Ginsburgh's School of the Soul, drawing her inspiration primarily from Chabad Chassidic writings. She studies and teaches Torah and Chassidus. Her previous book 'A Feminine Journey Through the Omer Count' has inspired many women.

nissim.name: Nissim Krispil
nissim.bio: A writer, researcher, and anthropologist, one of the pioneers in the field of ethnobotany in Israel. For decades, he has been documenting the healing traditions, faith, and culture of Jewish and folk communities in Israel and around the world. His books, dealing with medicinal plants, foraging, folklore, and ethnography, have become milestones in Israeli research and culture. The crown jewel among them is the comprehensive encyclopedia 'Yalkut HaTzemaḥim' (The Plant Collection). Alongside his research work, Nissim also engages in poetry and music, seeing nature and humanity as one complete unity, seeking to connect body and soul, science and faith, earth and spirit.

tamar.name: Tamar Eshel
tamar.title: Homeopath, Student and Teacher of Inner Torah
tamar.bio: A lawyer by training, but her heart is devoted to the world of kindness and natural medicine. She studied homeopathy at the school of Yosef Reves, the father of homeopathy in Israel, and for over two decades has been practicing homeopathy and the connection between body, soul, and faith. A graduate of Rabbi Yitzchak Ginsburgh's School of the Soul, drawing her inspiration primarily from Chabad Chassidic writings. She studies and teaches Torah and Chassidus. Her previous book 'A Feminine Journey Through the Omer Count' has inspired many women.

together.title: Together, Creating Magic
together.description: Together, the two create Nes HaTamar — a book that bridges seemingly distant worlds: Torah and homeopathy, tradition and natural medicine, art and science. This is a work in which body and soul, earth and heavens, miracle and nature — unite into one healing journey.

course.title: Introduction to Homeopathy Course
course.subtitle: With Tamar Eshel - Attorney, Veteran Homeopath, and Teacher of Homeopathy and the Inner Wisdom of Torah

about.title: About the Course
about.description: The course will include 8 fascinating 2-hour sessions in the morning hours, once a week starting in February. It will expose students to the healing powers inherent in the three kingdoms - plant, animal, and mineral. The course will pave the way to sustainable, robust health through the long and short path, with a special connection to the Holy Torah and Jewish thought throughout the generations.
